name: Indonesia Open

venue.name: Istora Senayan
venue.blurb: The cathedral of Indonesian badminton — built in 1961, refreshed in 2018, home to the Indonesia Open since 1982. Few arenas turn the crowd into a 13th player like this one.

stakes: This Super 1000 event is a crucial proving ground for India's top shuttlers, offering vital ranking points and a chance to assert dominance against the world's elite as the season progresses.
summary:
India's badminton contingent arrived at the POLYTRON Indonesia Open 2026 with a mix of high hopes and challenging draws. Men's Doubles third seeds Satwiksairaj Rankireddy & Chirag Shetty lead the charge, fresh off their Singapore Open triumph, looking to defend their 2023 Indonesia Open title and continue their dominant form. Their R32 clash against Denmark's Astrup and Rasmussen is a stern test right out of the gate.

In Men's Singles, eighth seed Lakshya Sen faces a formidable opponent in Thailand's Kunlavut Vitidsarn, a rematch of last year's All England quarter-final. Sen will be keen to bounce back after a frustrating Singapore Open exit where he received a yellow card. Meanwhile, HS Prannoy also has a tough opener against defending champion Anders Antonsen from Denmark. On the women's side, PV Sindhu successfully navigated her opening round, advancing to the Round of 16, a positive step in her "rebuild" at this Super 1000 event.

Other Indian hopefuls include Women's Doubles pair Treesa Jolly & Gayatri Gopichand, who are in career-best form but face a strong Korean duo in Kim and Kong. Mixed Doubles pair Dhruv Kapila & Tanisha Crasto drew the world No. 1 Chinese pair Zheng and Huang, while Malvika Bansod has arguably the toughest opener against Japan's Akane Yamaguchi. The depth of the draw at a Super 1000 event means every match is a battle, and India's shuttlers will need to be at their absolute best to make a deep run.
